commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e65024d99317d6a7c25524970cec3c9fb8ba9b36)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a TUI `loadHistory` early return for `historyLimit === 0` and a focused unit test that expects no gateway history call.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +39. Total +48 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection for the lower-level TUI options path: `createSessionActions` forwards `historyLimit: 0` to `chat.history`, whose protocol schema requires `limit >= 1`. The public command in the PR body is not reproduced end to end because current CLI parsing rejects zero before `runTui`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer/product judgment on changing the public zero-limit contract plus contributor proof and a rebase, not a safe standalone automated repair.

target.title: fix(tui): handle zero history limit
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes `openclaw tui --history-limit 0` so it disables history loading locally instead of sending an invalid `chat.history` request to the gateway.

Before this change, the TUI passed `limit: 0` through to the gateway, which rejected it because history limits must be at least 1.

## Changes

- Treat `historyLimit === 0` as an explicit no-history mode.
- Clear the local chat log and show the session marker without calling gateway history loading.
- Add regression coverage for the zero-history path.

## Testing

- `pnpm vitest run src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`

## Notes

AI-assisted contribution. I reproduced the issue locally with `openclaw tui --history-limit 0` and verified the fix locally.
target.author: stedyclaw
